Author: anfroholic
Content:
So I think I'm happy enough with this design. 
Again, it seems we need to have a thing on the bottom that pushes up the die from the bottom off the tape. 
It also has a vacuum to keep the tape pulled down while it pushes up. 
It's actuated by a stepper motor with some swash plates.

Author: anfroholic
Content: Lots and lots of printing. I am hoping to cut the real carousel and feeder backs tomorrow and assemble in the next few days

Author: anfroholic
Content: made the vacuum pusher thing that pokes the dies off the tape today. I think it works pretty well (it's not complicated)

Content: I think when I did my original count I was using neopixels as the reference. These dies are much shorter so fit significantly more on the reel.

Author: anfroholic
Content:
Hey all, sorry for not making many posts, been busy building. 

First up, we have a Ton of feeders. 
When you say, oh yeah, it's just 40, but time adds up fast when there are that many. There's about 2k components in the BOM for all these.

Author: anfroholic
Content:
And for the machine,
It's actually more like 2 machines, one below with the suction pusher thing that releases the dies from the tape (it's pretty sticky)
and another from above that will pick and place the dies. 
There's the carousel over to the right that will hold 40 feeders and rotates to index each feeder. 
-# please excuse the mess

More coming very soon

Author: mithro_
Content: @Andrew Wingate - Random question, does the machine pick all dies for a given slot first and then all for the next slot, or does it pick all dies in a reticle shot?

Author: anfroholic
Content: I'm having it go slot by slot as changing feeders is an expensive (in time) operation

Author: mithro_
Content: @Andrew Wingate - With the rotation changing thingy for the feeders, I guessed that was how it worked.

Author: nmz787
Content: @Andrew Wingate do you plan to integrate any microprobes for inline testing? A simple perimeter resistor that hugs the seal ring can tell a lot about the health of the die (has dicing cracked it, how far outside of the standard deviation is the resistance which can extend to circuit timing skew, or gross shorts/opens). You'd need a kelvin connection (four terminals).

Author: anfroholic
Content:
There will be no testing on individual dies as they are all different and that would be very difficult. 

We are making some test boards for a select couple dies that will be used to prove the wirebonding.

Author: nmz787
Content: I thought the dies from waferspace are all identical

Author: nmz787
Content: from a given wafer

Author: nmz787
Content: 4 probes and an ohmmeter is too difficult?

Author: nmz787
Content: sort test has been done for 50 years

Author: anfroholic
Content:
Every project is unique. 
Most projects share the same pad ring (power and IO locations)
All projects share the same wirebonding pattern* (not perfectly true, but true enough for this argument)

Author: nmz787
Content: maybe I am confusing tinytapeout dies

Author: nmz787
Content: but in any case, "probe cards"

Author: nmz787
Content: or wafer bonding style microprobes

Author: anfroholic
Content:
All these processes are still very new, we're creating them as we go. 
This is a good idea and will bring it up with the bonding house, but for this run most everything is still experimental.

Author: nmz787
Content: all very automatable

Author: anfroholic
Content: automatability does not mean no work and no cost

Author: nmz787
Content: right, just better yield to customers

Author: nmz787
Content: idk what the cost savings would be to skip bonding bad dies

Author: nmz787
Content: vs development cost of inline test

Author: anfroholic
Content:
This is a very proven node (GF180MCU) we are not anticipating many bad dies. 
Additionally as these are all on mezzanine connectors you are free to make your own test motherboards and do any number of tests before you attach them to something else.

Author: anfroholic
Content: not that much

Author: anfroholic
Content:
I understand your argument more now.
No, the sorter is only for taking dies from the diced wafer and putting them into their own SMT reels.

Author: anfroholic
Content: From there, some only requested the bare dies and they will wirebond (or whatever they like) themselves
